description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Facilities with storage tanks (receiving tanks) larger than 10 cubic meters in capacity are classified as "simple exclusive water supply systems" and are required by the Water Supply Law to be properly maintained by the facility owner.

Owners and managers of applicable facilities must regularly perform the following:

• Cleaning of storage tanks (receiving tanks): at least once per year
• Water quality testing: once per year, conducted by a registered testing organization

Proper hygiene management of storage tank water supplies is important to ensure that safe water produced at water treatment plants is delivered appropriately to each household. Even for small-scale storage tank water supplies with a capacity of 10 cubic meters or less, please make efforts to perform similar maintenance.

If you have your storage tank cleaned or water quality tested, you do not need to report this to the Water Works Division.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/jogesuidobu/suidokomuka/13444.html
summary: ℹ️ Proper maintenance of storage tanks (receiving tanks) for water supply
